summary: Round-1 blockers are closed: ChatViewModel.send now snapshots _attachments, extracts images/audioClips with the correct types, clears the state atomically, and calls LlmModelHelper.runInference with the signature-matching parameters (List<Bitmap>, List<ByteArray>). Attachment.id via AtomicLong, ThumbnailStrip keyed by attachment.id, MultimodalInputBar collapsed to (state, callbacks, modifier) with @Immutable/@Stable, rememberSaveable for draft text, dead InputBarHorizontalPadding / rememberMultimodalInputText removed, FQN imports fixed, send() trim centralised. Security-auditor low findings (TOCTOU inside _attachments.update, R5 downscale in addImageBitmap) verified. However, the callback holder refactor introduced one new major regression in ChatScreen.ReadyContent: `attachments` is captured inside `remember { MultimodalInputCallbacks(...) }` without keys, so the `onSend` guard reads a stale list from first composition — attachment-only send (blank text + attached image) silently drops the Send tap. Text-only send still works because `text.isNotBlank()` short-circuits before the stale `attachments.isNotEmpty()` is evaluated, which is why tests/manual smoke likely missed this.

findings.issue: Regression introduced by the round-1 callback-holder refactor. `val callbacks = remember { MultimodalInputCallbacks(onSend = { if (text.isNotBlank() || attachments.isNotEmpty()) { ... } }, ...) }` has no key list, so the factory runs exactly once. The `onSend` lambda captures `attachments` (a plain `List<Attachment>` local val, not a State/delegated property) from the very first composition, where the list is always empty. On later recompositions `attachments` refreshes in outer scope, but the remembered callbacks holder still closes over the stale first-composition reference. Consequence: when the user attaches an image but types no text, `MultimodalInputBar` enables Send (because the separately-computed `inputState.hasAttachments` is fresh), but tapping it evaluates `text.isNotBlank() || attachments.isNotEmpty()` as `false || false` → `sendRef.value(text)` is never called. AC-9/AC-13 attachment-only send is broken. Text-only send still works (short-circuit on `text.isNotBlank()`), which masks the bug in most manual walkthroughs and in unit tests that only exercise the VM directly. Note: `text` reads fine because `var text by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf("") }` captures the MutableState via the property delegate, not the current value — that path is correct. `sendRef` / `stopRef` / `pickImagesRef` are safe because `rememberUpdatedState` returns a live State. `attachments` is the only stale capture.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap `attachments` the same way the callables are wrapped: `val attachmentsRef = rememberUpdatedState(attachments)` above the remember block, then inside the lambda read `attachmentsRef.value.isNotEmpty()`. Alternative: drop the guard inside `onSend` entirely — `MultimodalInputBar`'s `canSend = (state.text.isNotBlank() || state.hasAttachments) && !state.isGenerating` already gates the button, so the lambda can unconditionally call `sendRef.value(text); text = ""` and let `ChatViewModel.send` re-check (it already does: `if (normalized.isEmpty() && pending.isEmpty()) return`). Preferred: remove the redundant guard, since ChatScreen shouldn't duplicate the VM's validation — same reasoning used to remove the trim duplication in round 1. Add a UI test (e.g. ChatScreenTest or Robolectric) that attaches an image with blank text and asserts send is invoked to prevent regression.
